story.iWant: YAML-based state files for tracking workflow progress
story.soThat: builds are recoverable and progress is human-readable

subtasks.subtask: Define status.yaml schema per tech spec (mode, current_slide, total_slides, built_count, last_action, last_modified, history[])
Create initial status.yaml with default values when first workflow runs
Implement ISO 8601 timestamp formatting for last_modified and history entries
Store status.yaml in `.slide-builder/` root directory
Create function to update status.yaml on state changes
Append action to history array with timestamp
Update last_action and last_modified fields
Preserve existing state when adding new entries (no overwrite)
Read existing status.yaml at workflow start
Detect interrupted workflow state (workflow started but not completed)
Offer user option to resume from last checkpoint or restart
Restore execution context from saved state
Use clear, descriptive field names (no abbreviations)
Add inline YAML comments for complex sections
Format history entries with readable action descriptions
Validate YAML remains parseable after writes
Define deck mode plan.yaml schema (deck_name, created, audience, purpose, slides[])
Define single mode plan.yaml schema (intent, suggested_template, created)
Place deck plan at `.slide-builder/deck/plan.yaml`
Place single plan at `.slide-builder/single/plan.yaml`
Run test workflow, verify status.yaml created correctly
Interrupt workflow, verify state preserved
Resume workflow, verify continuation from checkpoint
Manually inspect YAML files for readability

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given a workflow is executing, when state changes occur (slide built, plan created, etc.), then the state is written to appropriate YAML file (status.yaml, plan.yaml)
State files include timestamps in ISO 8601 format and action descriptions
Given a previous workflow was interrupted, when the user resumes, then the system reads the state file and continues from last checkpoint
Given a user wants to inspect progress, when they open any state YAML file, then the content is human-readable with clear structure

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Defines status.yaml schema (mode, current_slide, total_slides, built_count, last_action, last_modified, history[]) and plan.yaml schemas for both deck and single modes.

doc.title: Slide Builder Architecture
doc.snippet: Specifies YAML format for status.yaml and plan.yaml with human-readable structure. Includes logging strategy where all significant actions are logged to status.yaml history array.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: NFR14 requires partial builds be recoverable. NFR17 requires state files be human-readable YAML for manual recovery if needed.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: User story definition and acceptance criteria for YAML-based state files tracking workflow progress with resume capability.

doc.title: Previous Story - Workflow Definition Pattern
doc.snippet: Test workflow created at .slide-builder/workflows/test/ - available for testing state management. Checkpoint pattern established with template-output tags.

file.reason: Test workflow available for validating state file creation and updates during execution
Contains 3 steps with checkpoint (template-output) and user input (ask) tags - ideal for testing state persistence at checkpoints

dependencies.note: No additional dependencies needed for state file management - YAML read/write handled by Claude Code native capabilities

constraints.constraint: All timestamps must be in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ)
State files must be human-readable YAML - users can manually inspect and recover from state files
Partial builds must be recoverable - state saved at checkpoints enables resume
State files are the audit trail - history array captures all significant actions
100% BMAD pattern alignment - state management follows BMAD workflow.xml patterns
State Manager must integrate with workflow executor - called at workflow start, after actions, at checkpoints, and at completion

interface.name: status.yaml
interface.signature: mode: deck | single | null current_slide: number total_slides: number built_count: number last_action: string last_modified: ISO8601 timestamp history: - action: string timestamp: ISO8601

interface.name: plan.yaml (deck mode)
interface.signature: deck_name: string created: YYYY-MM-DD audience: string purpose: string slides: - number: integer intent: string template: string status: pending | built

interface.name: plan.yaml (single mode)
interface.signature: intent: string suggested_template: string created: ISO8601

interface.name: State Manager Integration Points
interface.signature: - Workflow start: Read existing state, initialize if needed - After action completion: Update last_action field - At template-output checkpoint: Save full state for resume - At workflow completion: Finalize state, add completion to history

tests.standards: Testing follows manual verification patterns from tech spec. Unit tests verify YAML schema compliance. Integration tests execute the test workflow and verify state file creation. Resume tests interrupt mid-workflow and verify continuation from checkpoint. Readability tests involve manual inspection of generated YAML files to ensure human-readable structure.

ideas.idea: Run test workflow, verify status.yaml created with correct schema fields
Check last_modified and history timestamps match ISO 8601 format
Interrupt test workflow at step 2 checkpoint, restart, verify resume option offered
Open status.yaml in text editor, verify field names are descriptive and structure is clear
Create deck plan, verify plan.yaml created at .slide-builder/deck/plan.yaml with correct schema
Create single slide plan, verify plan.yaml created at .slide-builder/single/plan.yaml with correct schema
